title: The Castaway.
content:
آ آ آ آ Obscurest night involved the sky,
آ آ آ آ The Atlantic billows roarï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ When such a destined wretch as I,
آ آ آ آ Washï؟½d headlong from on board,
آ آ آ آ Of friends, of hope, of all bereft,
آ آ آ آ His floating home for ever left.


آ آ آ آ No braver chief could Albion boast
آ آ آ آ Than he with whom he went,
آ آ آ آ Nor ever ship left Albionï؟½s coast
آ آ آ آ With warmer wishes sent.
آ آ آ آ He loved them both, but both in vain,
آ آ آ آ Nor him beheld, nor her again.


آ آ آ آ Not long beneath the whelming brine,
آ آ آ آ Expert to swim, he lay;
آ آ آ آ Nor soon he felt his strength decline,
آ آ آ آ Or courage die away:
آ آ آ آ But waged with death a lasting strife,
آ آ آ آ Supported by despair of life.


آ آ آ آ He shouted; nor his friends had failï؟½d
آ آ آ آ To check the vesselï؟½s course,
آ آ آ آ But so the furious blast prevailï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ That, pitiless perforce,
آ آ آ آ They left their outcast mate behind,
آ آ آ آ And scudded still before the wind.


آ آ آ آ Some succour yet they could afford;
آ آ آ آ And, such as storms allow,
آ آ آ آ The cask, the coop, the floated cord,
آ آ آ آ Delayed not to bestow:
آ آ آ آ But he, they knew, nor ship nor shore,
آ آ آ آ Whateï؟½er they gave, should visit more.


آ آ آ آ Nor, cruel as it seemï؟½d, could he
آ آ آ آ Their haste himself condemn,
آ آ آ آ Aware that flight, in such a sea,
آ آ آ آ Alone could rescue them;
آ آ آ آ Yet better felt it still to die
آ آ آ آ Deserted, and his friends so nigh.


آ آ آ آ He long survives, who lives an hour
آ آ آ آ In ocean, self-upheld:
آ آ آ آ And so long he, with unspent power,
آ آ آ آ His destiny repellï؟½d:
آ آ آ آ And ever, as the minutes flew,
آ آ آ آ Entreated help, or criedï؟½ï؟½Adieu!ï؟½


آ آ آ آ At length, his transient respite past,
آ آ آ آ His comrades, who before
آ آ آ آ Had heard his voice in every blast,
آ آ آ آ Could catch the sound no more:
آ آ آ آ For then, by toil subdued, he drank
آ آ آ آ The stifling wave, and then he sank.


آ آ آ آ No poet wept him; but the page
آ آ آ آ Of narrative sincere,
آ آ آ آ That tells his name, his worth, his age,
آ آ آ آ Is wet with Ansonï؟½s tear;
آ آ آ آ And tears by bards or heroes shed
آ آ آ آ Alike immortalize the dead.


آ آ آ آ I therefore purpose not, or dream,
آ آ آ آ Descanting on his fate,
آ آ آ آ To give the melancholy theme
آ آ آ آ A more enduring date:
آ آ آ آ But misery still delights to trace
آ آ آ آ Its semblance in anotherï؟½s case.


آ آ آ آ No voice divine the storm allayï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ No light propitious shone;
آ آ آ آ When, snatchï؟½d from all effectual aid,
آ آ آ آ We perishï؟½d, each alone:
آ آ آ آ But I beneath a rougher sea,
آ آ آ آ And whelmï؟½d in deeper gulfs than he.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "The Castaway."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
